meaning: n.分割,划分,瓜分,分开,隔离物vt.区分,隔开,分割
examples: For example, DB2 supports massively parallel processing (MPP) architecture and can run on a single partition (EE) or multiple partitions (EEE).
The uncle had made a wooden partition here for the goats, and covered the floor with straw.
Germany's seizure of Austria and the Munich Pact for the partition of Czechoslovakia (1938) also aroused the American people.
He is also pushing for Israel to agree to a de facto partition of Jerusalem.
The Albanians reject this policy on the grounds that it would amount to Kosovo's partition.
